commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: Massive blocks of unused imports (Container, Card, Button, etc.) in multiple sub-component files.
issues.fix: Remove unused imports from WeatherMain.tsx and WeatherStats.tsx to keep the bundle clean.

issues.description: Inconsistent spacing implementation; WeatherHeader uses CSS variables while ForecastStrip uses hardcoded numeric values for gaps.
issues.fix: Replace numeric gaps (e.g., gap={16}) with design system tokens like gap="var(--ggui-spacing-4)".

issues.description: Hardcoded font size (5rem) for the main temperature bypasses the design system's typography tokens.
issues.fix: Use a design token for sizing or a specific Heading variant if available to ensure scale consistency.

issues.description: The 5-day forecast strip is a list of data but is not marked up as a semantic list (ul/li).
issues.fix: Wrap the forecast items in a list structure or add role="list" and role="listitem" to the rows/stacks.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly functional and visually well-balanced, successfully implementing all requested features including the 5-day forecast. The separation of logic into a custom hook and sub-components is excellent, though the code contains significant import bloat and some inconsistencies in how spacing tokens are applied.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid data handling and hierarchy, but suffers from inconsistent spacing usage (raw pixel values like `gap={4}`, `gap={16}` mixed with CSS variables) and missing design token rigor in several places. Polish is adequate but could benefit from hover states on forecast days and better visual feedback for interactive elements.
